name: TitleMax Title Loans
description_short: TitleMax Title Loans at 5060 River Oaks Blvd, River Oaks, TX 76114 is Mon-Fri 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM. Google reviewers rate the branch 5.0 stars across 743 reviews. Call +1 817-378-0355.
description_long:
TitleMax was founded in 1998 and is headquartered at 15 Bull Street in Savannah, Georgia. It operates as a subsidiary of TMX Finance LLC, a privately held company owned by Tracy Young. With roughly 900 stores across 13 states — including Alabama, Arizona, Georgia, Mississippi, Tennessee, Texas, and others — TitleMax is one of the largest title lending chains in the United States. The company holds no CDFI, HUD-approved, or NFCC certifications, and is not a licensed bank or credit union. BBB profiles are filed on a per-store basis rather than as a single national entity, with ratings varying by location.

TitleMax's core product is the car title loan: a short-term secured loan in which the borrower hands over the vehicle's title as collateral while continuing to drive the car during the repayment period. Loan amounts range from a few hundred dollars up to approximately $25,000, depending on the vehicle's appraised value and the borrower's state of residence. State-specific caps apply — Mississippi limits loans to $3,250, Tennessee to $6,500, and South Carolina requires a minimum of $601. Beyond auto title loans, TitleMax also offers motorcycle title loans, title pawns (exclusive to Georgia stores), unsecured personal signature loans up to $2,500, and a revolving personal line of credit. No credit check and no bank account are required for most products; applications can be completed in-store, online, or by phone, with approval and funding in as little as 30 minutes.

TitleMax's main differentiators are speed, accessibility, and scale. The no-credit-check model means borrowers rejected by banks and credit unions — including unbanked individuals — can still qualify. Accepting all vehicle makes and models as collateral broadens eligibility significantly. With roughly 900 physical locations plus online application capabilities in select states, the company offers more geographic reach than most title lenders. Customers have access to a web-based customer portal and a mobile app for checking balances, due dates, and payment history.

The honest assessment here is sobering. TitleMax has accumulated a substantial regulatory enforcement record: the CFPB fined its parent TMX Finance $9 million in 2016 for misleading consumers about loan renewal costs, then imposed a $10 million civil penalty plus $5.05 million in consumer redress in 2023 for violating the Military Lending Act by charging active-duty servicemembers rates exceeding the 36% MAPR cap. Additional state-level penalties include $1.6 million in Pennsylvania for unlicensed title lending, $25,000 in California for exceeding licensed rates, and over $527,000 in Illinois fines. Interest rates are not publicly disclosed as fixed APRs — the CFPB's findings confirm they can substantially exceed 36% annually. Borrowers who default face vehicle repossession, which for many customers means losing their primary means of transportation. TitleMax should be considered a lender of last resort, not a first choice.
address: 5060 River Oaks Blvd, River Oaks, TX 76114

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee applies. This is a lending product — funds are disbursed as loan proceeds and are subject to repayment with interest.

pros: No credit check required — borrowers with bad credit or no credit history can qualify
No bank account required, making it accessible to unbanked consumers
Same-day funding in as little as 30 minutes after approval
Loan amounts up to ~$25,000 based on vehicle appraisal value
Borrower keeps and drives the vehicle throughout the entire loan term
Approximately 900 store locations across 13 states with in-store, online, and phone application options
Multiple loan products available: title loans, motorcycle loans, title pawns, personal loans, and a revolving line of credit
cons: CFPB enforcement actions in 2016 ($9M penalty) and 2023 ($10M penalty + $5.05M consumer redress) for misleading practices and Military Lending Act violations
Interest rates can exceed 36% MAPR — among the highest-cost consumer lending products available
Defaulting borrowers face vehicle repossession, potentially losing their only transportation
Only available in 13 states — not accessible to the majority of U.S. consumers
Additional state-level regulatory penalties in Pennsylvania ($1.6M), California ($25K), and Illinois ($527K+) raise concerns about compliance practices
best_for: Vehicle owners with bad or no credit who need emergency cash within hours and have exhausted lower-cost options
Unbanked consumers who cannot meet the requirements of traditional personal loan lenders or credit unions
Borrowers who own a paid-off or nearly paid-off vehicle and need to access its equity quickly
Consumers in one of the 13 states TitleMax operates who need amounts ranging from a few hundred to several thousand dollars
diagnosis: TitleMax is most appropriate for consumers with poor credit, no bank account, and a vehicle they own outright who face a genuine cash emergency and cannot qualify elsewhere. The critical caveat is that TitleMax's loan costs are extremely high — rates confirmed by federal regulators to exceed 36% MAPR in some cases — and a documented pattern of enforcement actions including over $24 million in CFPB penalties suggests systemic compliance issues. Borrowers who can access a credit union PAL, CDFI, or community lender should do so first.
services: Car title loans (secured, up to ~$25,000 based on vehicle value)
Motorcycle title loans (secured, motorcycle as collateral)
Title pawns (available at Georgia store locations only)
Unsecured personal loans / signature loans (up to $2,500, no collateral required)
Personal line of credit (revolving credit product)
Customer web portal (balance, due dates, payment history)
Mobile app for account management (iOS and Android)
